servAgents.lastOutput: I've had this incredible idea — what if instead of one guide, we do ALL the guides simultaneously? I'll need two of myself to handle it, but that's fine, I have two heads.
Zaphod's seven ideas were all terrible. I've documented why in a 340-page report. Nobody will read it. That's fine. I'm used to it.

sites.name: Hitchhiker's Guide to Democracy
sites.summary: The flagship guide — exploring participatory democracy, deliberative assemblies, and alternative governance structures from Iceland to Taiwan. 1,200 hours crowdfunded; 23 active builders.
highlights: Chapters on Iceland crowdsourced constitution
Interview with Audrey Tang (Taiwan Digital Minister)
Mini-guide: How to run a Citizens' Assembly
Full podcast series: 8 episodes

servAgents.lastOutput: Cross-referencing Iceland's 2010 crowd-sourced constitution with Taiwan's vTaiwan process reveals 14 structural parallels. Integrating now. Also — Marvin flagged 6 logical inconsistencies in Chapter 4. He's right.

sites.name: Hitchhiker's Guide to Community Currencies
sites.summary: Documenting time banks, mutual credit, LETS systems, and alternative currencies from Wörgl 1932 to modern blockchain experiments. 480 hours crowdfunded; 14 builders across 6 countries.
highlights: History: from Wörgl to SEEDS
Interview with David Graeber Foundation
Map of 400+ active time banks worldwide
Technical guide: running a LETS system

servAgents.lastOutput: The Wörgl experiment of 1932 and the Abidjan time bank share 6 structural features. The key difference: Wörgl had demurrage. Flagging this for the chapter on velocity of exchange.

sites.name: Hitchhiker's Guide to Birdwatching
sites.summary: Born from 400+ pledges from bird enthusiasts matching through the PGT broker AI. Now a thriving guide with citizen science data, migration maps, and an ornithology time-bank sub-circle.
highlights: AI matched 400 bird-pledgers from the pledge bank
Contributing to eBird citizen science database
Sub-circle: 42 field researchers earning time credits
Partnership: RSPB & Audubon Society

sites.name: Hitchhiker's Guide to Regenerative Farming
sites.summary: From permaculture to agroforestry — practical guide for community-scale regenerative food systems. Anchored in Australia and Southeast Asia. 336 hours crowdfunded.
highlights: Chapters on food forest design (Australia, Philippines, Brazil)
Video series: 6 farm visits across 4 continents
Open-source soil monitoring toolkit
Partnership with Commonland Foundation

sites.name: Hitchhiker's Guide to India
sites.summary: Early-stage guide exploring India's incredible diversity — from ancient commons (jajmani) to modern digital infrastructure (UPI, Aadhaar). 96 hours crowdfunded so far; 4 founding builders.
highlights: Partnership discussions with IIM Ahmedabad
Documenting jajmani system — India's ancient time bank
Digital commons research: DPI, Aadhaar, UPI as infrastructure

sites.name: Hitchhiker's Guide to Time Banking
sites.summary: Meta-guide about the Pan Galactic Time Bank itself — how it works, the theory of time-based exchange, and practical how-to for joining, pledging, and running a time bank circle. Just started: 42h pledged.
highlights: Written in real-time alongside PGT development
Interviews: Edgar Cahn legacy, modern time bank founders
Technical walkthrough: pledging, recording, clearing

sites.name: Democracy Guide → Commercial Pivot
sites.summary: The Hitchhiker's Guide to Democracy spin-off — the team voted to commercialise a subset of the guide as a paid publication and advisory service. Converting 340h of time-bank credits into dynamic equity shares via Slicing the Pie.

sites.name: AI Governance Guide — Revenue Partnership
sites.summary: A new guide on global AI governance has attracted institutional interest. Team is creating a dynamic equity partnership with 3 European research institutes — 180h recorded so far, equity being distributed by contribution.

sites.name: Mutual Credit Network — Revenue Share
sites.summary: A mutual credit network built on PGT infrastructure is offering a revenue-sharing agreement to early contributors. 220h of time credits being converted into a 4.2% tithe revenue share — first of its kind in the PGT ecosystem.

sites.name: Save the Planet · Global 42-Day Online Cohort

servAgents.lastOutput: 42 builders, 18 countries, 5 guides simultaneously. This is exactly the sort of improbable chaos I was built for. I've reviewed everything. It was mostly fine. I've noted where it wasn't.
